data.title: Goldman Sachs (GS) Flaunts Top Dividend Growth Stock Status
data.description:

data.title: Rivian Stock (RIVN) Soars 28% — What Drove Its Biggest Rally? Analysts Weigh In
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (RIVN), the electric-vehicle maker, jumped about 28% on Friday, its biggest one-day gain on record, after investors reacted to its fourth-quarter results, 2026 outlook, and updates on the upcoming R2 vehicle.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The rally followed better-than-expected quarterly results and a wave of bullish analyst reactions. Wedbush analyst Dan Ives maintained an Outperform rating and $25 price target, calling the launch of the lower-cost R2 SUV "crucial."

Meanwhile, UBS upgraded Rivian to Neutral from Sell and raised its price target to $16, saying the stock's risk-reward now looks more balanced at current levels. Also, Deutsche Bank upgraded the shares to Buy from Neutral, pointing to confidence in Rivian's product pipeline and 2026 guidance.

What Stood Out This Quarter

The company reported about $1.29 billion in revenue and a smaller-than-expected loss per share of $0.70. It also posted a second straight quarter of gross profit. However, investors focused more on the company's outlook, especially updates on its upcoming R2 vehicle and delivery guidance.

Rivian said its lower-priced R2 SUV remains on track for customer deliveries in the second quarter of 2026. Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe called the launch a "key inflection" for the company, since the vehicle is meant to reach a much larger group of buyers than its current R1T pickup and R1S SUV.

The company also guided 2026 deliveries to about 62,000 to 67,000 vehicles, pointing to growth after a largely flat year. For EV makers, delivery outlook often matters more than a single quarter's earnings because it shows future demand. A more affordable vehicle could help increase production and improve manufacturing efficiency as Rivian expands output.

At the same time, the results showed some operating progress. Rivian recorded gross profit for a second straight quarter, helped in part by software and services revenue linked to its partnership with Volkswagen (VWAGY).

This indicates the company is beginning to generate income beyond vehicle sales, which investors often watch as a sign of improving business stability.

What Investors Will Watch Next

Looking ahead, the focus shifts to execution. Rivian still expects adjusted EBITDA losses of about $1.8 billion to $2.1 billion in 2026 and plans around $2 billion in spending as it prepares for the R2 launch.

Investors will watch whether the company can ramp production smoothly and meet its delivery guidance of 62,000 to 67,000 vehicles. The early stages of the R2 rollout matter, since higher volume is needed to lower costs per vehicle.

They will also track cash use and margins in the coming quarters. The recent gross profit and rising software and services revenue were positive signs, but the key question is whether higher production can slowly reduce losses and move the company toward profit.

Is RIVN Stock a Buy or Sell? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on RIVN stock based on seven Buys, four Holds, and five Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average RIVN price target of $17.75 per share implies 26.8% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Is Gold Up Today, 2/13/26?
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD) is up by over 2% on Friday and has reclaimed the $5,000 level after a cooler-than-expected inflation print led to rising rate cut odds. Gold tends to benefit when rates are lower, as the opportunity cost of holding the precious metal relative to interest-bearing assets, like Treasuries, decreases.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On an annual basis, the Consumer Price Index (CPI) fell to 2.4% in January from 2.7% in December, marking the lowest rate of growth since May 2025. January's core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, was 2.5%, the lowest level since March 2021.

Gold Rebounds as Rate Cut Hopes Grow

While a rate cut at the next Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting on March 18 is unlikely, the odds of a 25 bps reduction at the April 29 meeting climbed to 26.4% from 23.9% a day ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool. The odds are even higher at the June 17 meeting, sitting at 52.3% from 48.9% yesterday.

Gold's move today "suggests the correction may have overshot, with bargain‑hunting and position‑adjustments now providing support," said ING Bank commodity strategist Ewa Manthey. The precious metal has returned 16.4% year-to-date and has completely recovered its losses over the past week.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Nebius (NBIS) Stock Bounces Back — But Crowd Wisdom Signals Caution
data.description:
Nebius Group (NBIS) stock closed 9% higher at $98.01 on Friday after briefly trading lower earlier in the session, as investors reacted to the AI cloud infrastructure provider's fourth-quarter results, which missed revenue estimates even though sales rose from a year ago. However, TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom data still shows investor caution toward the shares.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Key Takeaways from Nebius' Q4

Nebius reported revenue of about $227.7 million for the quarter, sharply higher than a year ago but below expectations. Losses of $173 million also widened from the year-ago quarter as the company increased spending to expand its data center capacity.

Management said demand for its AI computing services remains strong. The company ended the year with about 170 megawatts of active power and plans to reach 800 megawatts to 1 gigawatt of connected capacity by the end of 2026. Nebius also reported annual recurring revenue of around $1.25 billion and posted a positive adjusted EBITDA quarter, showing higher usage of its platform.

However, investors appeared focused on costs and execution. Operating expenses climbed significantly as the company invested in new data center infrastructure, and the business is still far from profitability.

What Does TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom Show?

TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom data shows caution among retail investors. Based on activity from more than 852,000 tracked portfolios, investors' sentiment toward Nebius is currently Negative.

About 2.3% of tracked portfolios hold NBIS shares, with investors allocating an average of 4.69% of their portfolios to the stock. However, recent activity shows investors have been reducing exposure. The number of portfolios holding NBIS rose slightly over the past seven days but declined about 1.8% over the last 30 days.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: CapEx Reset Reshapes Bullish Thesis on Amazon Stock (AMZN)
data.description:
It hasn't been smooth sailing for Amazon (AMZN) investors in recent months. In fact, since last February, the Seattle-based company has been the worst performer within the so-called "Magnificent 7″ group of companies. Concerns over the growing amount of capital being allocated to AI infrastructure to sustain AWS growth have gained traction, especially with the possibility of negative free cash flow in 2026 if the business continues to grow at its current pace while investment spending accelerates.

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Advance on Optimistic Inflation Data ahead of Extended Holiday Weekend
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed higher on Friday ahead of a three-day weekend for the stock market in honor of Presidents' Day. The market will reopen on Tuesday, February 17.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Investors received an encouraging inflation update after the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) announced that January's Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose by 0.2% from the prior month, undercutting the consensus estimate of 0.3%. CPI rose by 2.4% year-over-year, also below the expectation of 2.5% and registering the slowest pace of growth since May 2025. Core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, increased by 0.3% month-over-month and 2.5% year-over-year, both of which were in line with the estimates.

Falling inflation supports the case for the Fed to lower interest rates. While the odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the March 18 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting remain low, the same odds at the next meeting on April 29 have risen to 26.4% from 23.9% a day ago on the CME FedWatch tool.

Meanwhile, the Pentagon published a document showing the addition of Alibaba (BABA), Baidu (BIDU), BYD (BYDDY), and several other Chinese companies to the Section 1260H list this morning. The list identifies firms that the Pentagon believes are associated with the Chinese military. However, the Pentagon took down the document about an hour later, with some speculating that the U.S. sought to ease tensions with Beijing ahead of the Trump-Xi summit in April.

"An agency letter requesting withdrawal of this document was received after placement on public inspection," wrote the Federal Register in an editorial note without providing additional details.

Elsewhere, the Trump administration is reportedly planning on easing tariffs on some aluminum and steel imports. President Trump imposed a 50% tariff on imports of the two metals last June. Since then, aluminum prices have surged 20%, while steel has climbed about 4%, adding to affordability concerns across the U.S.

However, White House trade adviser Peter Navarro denied that the U.S. would lower the tariffs, saying that Trump would maintain a "no exemptions, no exclusions" policy. Furthermore, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told CNBC that "If anything is done, I think it would be some sort of clarification on some incidental objects, but again, that's going to be the president's decision."

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.05% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.18%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: John Deere (DE) Taps the AI Boom to Drive the Future of Farming
data.description:
When people talk about AI stocks, farm equipment probably isn't the first sector that springs to mind. Agriculture has been around for roughly 12,000 years, after all — yet it remains just as vital to global food security today as it ever was. The industry is often associated with crops and livestock, not cutting-edge technology. But that perception is changing fast.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Farming is in the midst of a quiet revolution, one driven by sustainability, automation, and data-powered decision making. And sitting firmly in the driver's seat of this transformation is Deere & Co. (DE) — the brand that turned "John Deere green" into a symbol recognized worldwide.

With its Q4 earnings report approaching, Deere's stock has been showing impressive strength, hovering near all-time highs even as broader markets struggle for momentum. A major catalyst behind that resilience is Deere's new generation of AI-powered autonomous tractors, which are pulling agriculture squarely into the 21st century.
data.author: Gerelyn Terzo

data.title: Robinhood's (HOOD) Crypto-Fueled Growth Faces Stern 2026 Test
data.description:
Robinhood Markets' (HOOD) fourth-quarter earnings paint the picture of a platform evolving into a more resilient and diversified financial business — yet one that remains closely tied to the momentum of crypto markets. The explosive 300% surge in cryptocurrency revenue seen in Q3 2025 jump-started a new growth phase for the company, but Q4 made clear that this success comes with volatility, as investor sentiment and Bitcoin's (BTC-USD) price action increasingly influence both revenue and valuation.

data.title: Bank of America (BAC) Raises CEO Moynihan's Compensation 17% to $41 Million
data.description:
Bank of America (BAC) CEO Brian Moynihan has gotten a hefty pay raise.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Moynihan's compensation for 2025 rose 17% year-over-year to $41 million. Bank of America's board of directors maintained Moynihan's base salary at $1.5 million with no cash bonus, while granting equity incentive awards totaling $39.5 million, according to a regulatory filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).

The compensation increase follows a year in which Bank of America, the second-largest U.S. bank, improved its financial performance with net income rising 13% to $30.5 billion and earnings per share (EPS) increasing 19% to $3.81. Revenue last year grew 7% to $113.1 billion, reflecting record net interest income at the financial institution.

Bank of America's Stock Performance

In raising Moynihan's compensation, Bank of America's board said that his leadership helped drive shareholder growth in 2025. BAC stock rose 25% in 2025, reaching a new all-time high during the year. This followed a 31% increase in 2024. The company's market capitalization increased 19% last year, maintaining its position as the second-largest U.S. bank by market value after JPMorgan Chase (JPM).

Moynihan's compensation includes restricted stock options that vest over 12 months, and stock options that vest over four years. Under Moynihan, Bank of America maintains a strong balance sheet with total assets of $3.4 trillion. Its loan balances grew 8% and its deposits increased 3% in 2025.

Is BAC Stock a Buy?

Bank of America's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 19 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 15 Buy and four Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average BAC price target of $61.64 implies 17.59% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole
